channel.title: Features on Cloud Share
channel.description: Recent content in Features on Cloud Share

item.title: Text Sharing
item.description:
Text Sharing link

Text sharing is the fastest way to send a snippet — a command, a password, a code block, or any plain text up to 10 MB — from one machine to another.

Sending text link

In the sender UI link

  1. Open the sender web UI (auto-opens at http://localhost:5000, or re-open via browser)
  2. Click the Text tab

📸 Screenshot needed: text-sharing-sender-tab.png Description: Sender web UI with the Text tab selected, showing the text area and Share button

item.title: File Transfer
item.description:
File Transfer link

Cloud Share can transfer individual files up to 100 MB — encrypted in transit with AES-256-GCM and verified on arrival with SHA-512.

Uploading a file link

In the sender UI link

  1. Open the sender web UI (http://localhost:5000)
  2. Click the File tab

📸 Screenshot needed: file-transfer-sender-tab.png Description: Sender web UI with the File tab selected, showing the drag-and-drop zone

  1. Either:
    • Drag and drop a file onto the drop zone
    • Click Browse (or the drop zone) to open a file picker

📸 Screenshot needed: file-transfer-sender-dragdrop.png Description: File tab with a file being dragged onto the drop zone, showing the hover state

item.title: Zip Bundles
item.description:
Zip Bundles link

The Zip Bundle feature lets you package multiple files into a single encrypted archive and send them as one queue item. You can optionally add an AES-256 zip-level password for an additional layer of protection on top of the transport encryption.

Creating a zip bundle link

In the sender UI link

  1. Open the sender web UI (http://localhost:5000)
  2. Click the Zip Bundle tab

📸 Screenshot needed: zip-bundles-sender-tab.png Description: Sender web UI with the Zip Bundle tab selected, showing the multi-file drop zone and password field

item.title: Encryption & Integrity
item.description:
Encryption & Integrity link

This page provides a complete technical description of how cloud-share encrypts every item and verifies its integrity on arrival.

Overview link

Every item queued by the sender goes through the following pipeline before being stored in memory:

flowchart TD
 A[Plaintext content] --> B[SHA-512 hash computed]
 A --> C["AES-256-GCM encrypt(key, nonce, plaintext)"]
 B --> D[Hash stored with item metadata]
 C --> E["Payload: nonce 12B || ciphertext || auth tag 16B"]
 E --> F[Base64-encode entire payload]
 F --> G[Stored in queue, served to receiver]

Key derivation link

The AES-256 encryption key is derived from the session secret using SHA-256:

item.title: Web UI
item.description:
Web UI link

Both cloud-share-sender and cloud-share-receiver include a built-in web UI that opens automatically in your default browser when you start the tool. The UI is built with Bootstrap 5, Alpine.js, and a dark theme — no installation required, everything is served by the tool itself (CDN resources loaded from jsDelivr).

Sender UI link

The sender UI is served at http://localhost:5000 (or your custom port).

📸 Screenshot needed: web-ui-sender-overview.png Description: Full sender web UI showing the tab bar (Text, File, Zip Bundle), the queue table below, and the dark theme

item.title: Real-Time Updates
item.description:
Real-Time Updates link

Cloud Share uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) to push queue changes from the sender to the receiver instantly — no polling, no page refresh.

How SSE works in cloud-share link

When the receiver UI loads, it opens a persistent HTTP connection to the sender’s event stream endpoint:

 GET /events?secret=<your-secret>

The sender keeps this connection open and pushes newline-delimited JSON events whenever the queue changes. The browser’s native EventSource API (used by Alpine.js) handles reconnection automatically.
